items.title: Grading the path, not the output
items.tell: Your grader fails an agent that got the right answer via a different tool sequence than expected.
items.fix: Grade the final outcome (was the ticket resolved?), not the trajectory. Reserve path checks for cases where a specific step is genuinely required (e.g., 'verify_identity must be called before process_refund').

items.title: Class-imbalanced data
items.tell: Your safety eval has 20 harmful prompts and zero benign ones. Now you can't tell if the model over-refuses.
items.fix: Include both sides. Test where the behavior should happen AND where it shouldn't. A one-sided eval produces one-sided optimization.

items.title: Rigid string match on numeric answers
items.tell: Expected '96.124991…', model returned '96.12'. Grader marks it wrong. The model was right.
items.fix: Use tolerance for numbers (abs diff < epsilon). For open-ended text, use LLM-as-judge with a rubric rather than exact-match.

items.title: Never reading transcripts
items.tell: Score dropped 5 points. You retrain, tune prompts, chase ghosts — but you never opened a single failed transcript.
items.fix: Sample ~10 failed transcripts per eval run. Half the time the grader is wrong, not the model. The other half you learn something a metric can't tell you.
